title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. homophylla
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. homophylla, also known as grey santolina or lavender cotton, is a perennial herb in the sunflower family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. homophylla include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate respiratory issues.
